rulings.Case Title: ELYN P BRENNAN VS. R STEPHEN GOLDSTEIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File A Cross Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, April 11, 2013, Line 3, DEFENDANT PELOSI, JR. PAUL FRANCIS Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File A Cross Complaint Denied, without prejudice. Mr. Pelosi moves for leave to file a Cross-Complaint pursuant to CCP § 473. (Notice of Motion, p. 1:26-27.) However, CCP § 473 is not the correct statute for seeking leave to file a Cross-Complaint. "As a general rule, the trial court may consider only the grounds stated in the notice of motion. [Citations.]" (Luri v. Greenwald (2003) 107 Cal.App.4th 1119, 1125.) Also, moving party should attach the proposed Cross-Complaint to his declaration. (Weil & Brown, Cal. Practice Guide: Civ. Proc. Before Trial, 6:561; see Pelosi Decl., p. 2:1-2.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: JOHNNIE L. BROWN VS. DENNIS J. HERRERA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn In Oppos To Ccsf'S Response To Pltf;S Req For Admissions
rulings.Rulings: Set for Hearing on Thursday, April 11, 2013, Line 5, PLAINTIFF JOHNNIE BROWN'S Mtn In Oppos To Ccsf'S Response To Pltf;S Req For Admissions Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Off calendar as untimely under CCP § 1005(b). The motion was filed and served on April 4, 2013. Moving party only provided 5 court days notice. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepa@cooley.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/jpt

rulings.Case Title: CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION, INC. DBA GREAT VS. HELPING HANDS SANCTUARY OF IDAHO, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, April 11, 2013, Line 6, DEFENDANT ROY PRESCOTT'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT The demurrer is sustained, with 10 days leave to amend, as to the second cause of action (breach of written guaranty). The demurrer is sustained, without leave to amend as to the first (breach of contract), third (services sold and performed); fourth (open account); fifth (account stated); sixth (quantum meruit); and seventh causes of action (unjust enrichment). In opposition, Plaintiff concedes the errors in its Second Amended Complaint as to these causes of action and proposes to file a Third Amended Complaint that: (1) has legible copies of the written contracts; (2) removes Mr. Prescott as a party to the first cause of action; and (3) eliminates the fifth, sixth and seventh causes of action in their entirety. Leave to amend is granted for Plaintiff to file the proposed Third Amended Complaint attached to its opposition. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: IDA CHRISTINA FUA CRUZ VS. JOEL ENRIQUE ANDINO SANCHEZ et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Ida Fua Cruz'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery
rulings.Rulings: Set for Hearing on Thursday, April 11, 2013, Line 4, Plaintiff Ida Fua Cruz'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. TWO IS DENIED. ADMITTEDLY, DEFENDANT KNOWS WHETHER OR NOT HE CONSIDERED HIMSELF AN EMPLOYEE OR INDEPENDENT CONTRACTOR, THAT BELIEF MAY BE ERRONEOUS AS A MATTER OF LAW AND AT THIS STAGE OF THE LITIGATION HE IS NOT REQUIRED TO CHOOSE BETWEEN THESE RELATIONSHIP WITH YELLOW CAB. THE ISSUE OF DEFENDANT JOEL ENRIQUE ANDINO SANCHEZ' RELATIONSHIP WITH YELLOW CAB IS ONE ON WHICH THE PLAINTIFF HAS THE BURDEN AND BY REASON OF DEFENDANT'S RESPONSE, PLAINTIFF IS PUT ON NOTICE THAT SHE WILL HAVE TO PRESENT EVIDENCE ON THIS ISSUE UNLESS SUBSEQUENT DISCOVERY ACTIVITIES OR MOTIONS RESOLVE IT IN ADVANCE OF TRIAL. rulings.Case Title: CATALINO CARINO et al VS. ALIREZA KATOUZIAN MD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Trnasfer Action To Ventura County Superior Court
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, April 11, 2013, Line 8, DEFENDANT SPANISH HILLS SURGERY CENTER'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Trnasfer Action To Ventura County Superior Court Motion to transfer venue is denied. Defendant has failed to meet its burden of showing as to that San Francisco is a seriously inconvenient location for non-party, non-party employee witnesses to appear, or why the ends of justice would promoted by transfer to Ventura County. Defendant presents declarations that were presented in its July 2012 motion, which was denied without prejudice, plus the declaration of Defendant Katouzian, who is a party. Plaintiff presents evident that three non-party physician witnesses would not be inconvenienced by a trial in San Francisco. Further, trial in this case is set for May 13, 2013. There would be a substantial trial delay if the case were transferred at this late date to Ventura County. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: CHRISTIAN ROETTGERS VS. AUTONET MOBILE, INC. A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for Hearing on Thursday, April 11, 2013, Line 2, DEFENDANT AUTONET MOBILE, INCORPORATED'S Motion For Protective Order Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: DEFENDANT AUTONET MOBILE, INC.'S MOTION FOR A PROTECTIVE ORDER IS DENIED. THIS MATTER IS PRESENTLY SET FOR TRIAL ON MAY 20, 2013 AND PLAINTIFF HAS THE RIGHT TO COMPLETE THE TAKING OF HIS DEPOSITION TO PREPARE FOR TRIAL. THERE HAS BEEN NO SHOWING OF GOOD CAUSE AS REQUIRED BY SECTION 2025.420(b) TO JUSTIFY THE ISSUANCE OF A PROTECTIVE. MR. PRATZ HAS HAD ALMOST ONE MONTH TO OBTAIN SEPARATE COUNSEL IF HE HAD SO CHOSEN TO DO SO AND HIS FAILURE TO DO SO IS NOT A BASIS FOR DENYING PLAINTIFF THE RIGHT TO COMPLETE THE DEPOSITION. IF THE COURT GRANT'S PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO AMEND THE COMPLAINT AND ADD MR. PRATZ AS A NAMED DEFENDANT UNDOUBTEDLY THE PRESENT TRIAL DATE WILL BE VACATED AND COUNSEL SHOULD BE ABLE TO WORK OUT A SCHEDULE THAT WILL ACCOMMODATE THE INTERESTS OF ALL PARTIES TO THIS LITIGATION. THE COURT WILL NOT IMPOSED ANY MONETARY SANCTIONS. COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFFS SHOULD PREPARE AND APPROPRIATE ORDER, SUBMIT TO COUNSEL FOR AUTONET FOR APPROVAL AS TO FORM AND SUBMIT TO THE COURT FOR SIGNATURE AT THE HEARING ON APRIL 11, 2013. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepa@cooley.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/jpt

rulings.Case Title: BANK OF THE WEST VS. BODY DETAILS, INC. A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, April 11, 2013, Line 13, DEFENDANTS BODY DETAILS, INC., ROBERT OZAKI'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT The demurrer is overruled in its entirety. Lack of personal jurisdiction and inconvenient forum are grounds for separate motions, not a basis for a demurrrer. CCP 418.10(a). In any event, they were not raised at the time of the filing of the first demurrer and are waived. CCP 418.10(e)(3). Further, the forum selection clause is not a basis to sustain the demurrer on the grounds of failure to state a cause of action under CCP 430.10(e). CCP 410.40, cited by Plaintiff, is not a threshold requirement and is inapplicable here. The demurrer to the fifth cause of action for claim and delivery is overruled because the Complaint is not uncertain and is not subject to heightened pleading requirements. The demurrer for lack of subject matter jurisdiction is overruled as Defendant has provided no authority to support that California courts have no jurisdiction over contracts such as the one at issue. This is Defendant's second demurrer to the Complaint; Defendant is now directed to file an answer to the Complaint within ten days of entry of this order. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: KAREN NIKOLAKAKIS VS. FRITZ PROPERTY GROUP, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To From Interrogatories And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, April 11, 2013, Line 14, PLAINTIFF KAREN NIKOLAKAKIS' Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To From Interrogatories And Request For Sanctions Granted. Fritz Property Group has selectively disclosed limited information about insurance coverage. Even if the coverage of the principal carrier disclosed by Fritz Property Group is adequate to satisfy a judgment for the plaintiff, it is beyond the province of the court to speculate about the role various potentially insurance coverage will play in the resolution of the case. Within 10 days, Fritz Property Group shall provide a further response to Form Interrogatories Set One, no. 4.1 by responding fully to the request with information regarding all insurance policies under Fritz Property Group, Inc. was insured or might be insured for the claims and damages arising from the incident alleged in Plaintiff's complaint, including primary, excess and umbrella policies. Monetary sanctions are denied. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: NO OPPOSITION TO DEFENDANTS, MOHAMMED AWADALLA AND KELLIE COUCH,S, MOTION TO COMPEL VERIFIED RESPONSES TO THEIR REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET ONE AND VERIFICATION OF THEIR REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION, SET ONE HAS BEEN FILED; ACCORDINGLY, THE MOTION WILL BE GRANTED AND PLAINTIFF IS ORDERED TO FILE VERIFICATION WITH 10 DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER. MONETARY SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $540. WILL BE ASSESSED AGAINST PLAINTIFF'S AND HIS ATTORNEY TO BE PAID WITHIN 10 DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepa@cooley.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/jpt

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, April 11, 2013, Line 16, PLAINTIFF GCFS, INC.'S, Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings Granted without leave to amend and judgment shall be entered in plaintiff's favor forthwith. No opposition filed. The Court will sign the proposed Order submitted with the moving papers. Plaintiff must prepare a separate judgment. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)
